brand.name: 2 Dogs Treats
brand.tagline: Not just a treat, a daily thank you

offer.what: Single-ingredient, human-grade dog treats and chews made in small batches in a Boston commercial kitchen — chicken, beef, turkey, pork, fish skins, organic sweet potatoes, and Summit chews. Also a Build-a-Box customization option and gift boxes.
offer.coreOutcome: Peace of mind: owners know exactly what their dog is eating; dogs with sensitivities, allergies, or pickiness finally have treats they love and thrive on.
offer.pricePoint: $13.95–$20.95 per product; Meat Lover's Box from $83.80; Gift Box $43.95; free shipping over $66; subscription option available
offer.commitment: One-time purchase or recurring subscription; no contract; satisfaction guarantee with full refund if dogs don't approve
leadMagnets: Email newsletter signup ('latest on sales, new releases and more')

audience.icp: Health-conscious dog owner, 30–55, treats their dog like family; prioritizes clean, transparent ingredients; likely has a dog with sensitivities, allergies, or a picky eater; mid-to-upper income, willing to pay premium for quality they can trust
painPoints: Fear of mystery ingredients and fillers in mainstream treats
Dog has allergies, digestive sensitivities, or food intolerances
Previous bad experience with imported/low-quality treats
Senior dog needs softer, easy-to-chew options
Puppy owner wants to start with clean, simple foods
segments: Dog owners with sensitive/allergy-prone dogs (single-protein elimination)
Senior dog owners needing soft-texture options (Chicken Rounds)
New puppy owners establishing healthy habits
Multi-dog households buying in bulk or subscription

salesCycle.length: Same day — impulse or considered DTC e-commerce purchase
salesCycle.trial: No free trial; however, a full satisfaction guarantee (refund if dog doesn't love it) removes purchase risk
salesCycle.closeMechanism: Self-serve e-commerce; product pages, Build-a-Box, social proof (1,162 reviews, 97% positive), and free-shipping threshold drive conversion
salesCycle.topObjection: Price premium vs. store-brand treats — 'why pay $20 for a bag of chicken strips?' Overcome by single-ingredient transparency, USA sourcing, human-grade quality, and the satisfaction guarantee

ahaMoment: The moment the dog goes crazy for the treat AND the owner flips the bag to find just one ingredient — validates both palatability and purity in one instant. Happens on first delivery; entirely self-serve.

retention.recurring: Subscription model available (frequency options exist, specific intervals not published); repeat purchase driven by 'same enthusiasm 9 years later' loyalty pattern
retention.expansion: Build-a-Box upsell to try new proteins; Summit chews as add-on; gift boxes for pet-owner gifting occasions; eGift card for referrals
retention.churnRisk: Switching to cheaper supermarket alternatives; dog loses interest in a single flavor (solved by variety); lapsed subscribers who forget to reorder

lifecycleGaps: No gated lead magnet — newsletter is the only opt-in, limiting top-of-funnel segmentation
Welcome & onboarding sequence: unknown/likely absent or thin
Post-purchase education sequence (ingredients, benefits, how to use) likely missing
Subscription conversion flow (nudging one-time buyers to subscribe) likely absent
Win-back / re-engagement campaign for lapsed buyers likely absent
Referral/advocacy program not surfaced on site
Renewal/anniversary or loyalty email not surfaced
toneVoice: Warm, personal, founder-led. Conversational and approachable — uses 'pup,' 'tail-wag approved,' 'woof.' Balances emotional dog-love storytelling (founder Mike Christian, dog Riley's origin story) with credible, educational ingredient transparency. Never clinical or salesy. Think: a knowledgeable dog-loving neighbor, not a pet-industry marketer.
compliance: Standard CAN-SPAM / GDPR email compliance. No HIPAA, financial, or legal constraints. Standard DTC e-commerce — include unsubscribe, physical address, honest claims about pet health (avoid disease-cure language per FTC/AAFCO pet marketing guidelines).
